title: The Longest Journey Sunlight Ever Takes Happens Before It Leaves The Sun - ScienceAlert
description: The Longest Journey Sunlight Ever Takes Happens Before It Leaves The Sun  ScienceAlertMost people think the Sun is a vast ball of burning gas, but that familiar picture is quietly wrong: the Sun is plasma, a fourth state of matter — atoms torn into charged particles, the same stuff as lightning and neon  Space DailyEnergy created in the Sun’s core can spend around 170,000 years bouncing through the radiative zone before reaching the outer layers — but once released as sunlight, it crosses 150 million kilometres to Earth in just over eight minutes  ScienceBlog.comYou never see the Sun as it is, only as it was: its light takes eight minutes and twenty seconds to cross the 150 million kilometres to Earth — so if the Sun blinked out, we would bask on, unknowing, for eight more minutes  ScienceBlog.comLight from the Sun takes about eight minutes to reach Earth, but the energy inside a single photon can spend tens of thousands of years bouncing through the Sun's dense core before it ever escapes the surface to begin that eight-minute trip  Space Daily
